solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def maxScoreIndices(self, nums: List[int]) -> List[int]:
        left_sum = 0
        right_sum = sum(nums)
        max_score = 0
        max_indices = []
        for i in range(len(nums)):
            if nums[i] == 0:
                left_sum += 1
            else:
                right_sum -= 1
            if left_sum + right_sum == max_score:
                max_indices.append(i)
            elif left_sum + right_sum > max_score:
                max_score = left_sum + right_sum
                max_indices = [i]
        return max_indices
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution iterates through the input array once, keeping track of the sum of zeros to the left and ones to the right of the current index. It updates the maximum score and the list of indices with the maximum score whenever it encounters a higher score. This approach has a time complexity of O(n) and a space complexity of O(1), making it efficient for large inputs.
